title: Gap Closure, Deliberate Practice, and Honest Positioning · Guided Evidence Sprint
summary: A guided career studio extends time-boxed role-readiness experiment plan while retaining truthful evidence, accessibility, privacy, security, consent, and learner agency. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of time-boxed role-readiness experiment plan while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Prioritize gaps by role frequency, task centrality, transfer distance, evidence weakness, learning cost, and application timing.
Design the smallest authentic task that can reveal or improve one missing behavior under a changed case.
Precommit a time, evidence, and opportunity-cost stop rule for readiness work.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# job-search cumulative learner-controlled career campaign
# Browser labs review fictional evidence contracts only. They never contact people,
# submit applications, access accounts, expose personal data, negotiate, or accept offers.
workspace: career
candidate-id: fictional-learner
target-role: [replace with role family, work outcomes, and bounded level]
jurisdiction: [replace with location and current official guidance boundary]
audience: [replace with reviewer need, time, format, and access context]
claim: [replace with one truthful role-relevant behavior claim]
evidence: [replace with revision, artifact, observation, changed case, contribution, and limit]
process-state: [replace with discover, investigate, apply, interview, offer, close, or archive]
accessibility: [replace with keyboard, structure, reflow, status, alternatives, and human review]
privacy-consent: [replace with purpose, minimization, permission, retention, deletion, and safe contact]
decision: [replace with learner-owned next decision and stop rule]
transfer-boundary: learner-approved external actions only
review:

steps.title: Commit to the mission model · Workshop Gap Priority
steps.instruction:
workshop case — Three genuine evidence gaps trigger a six-month general course plan even though only one may block the candidate’s target work. The workshop variation 4 changes the candidate background, target role, jurisdiction, access need, source date, process state, and decision constraint. Competency probe: defend one bounded career decision

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Commit to the mission model · Workshop Gap Priority.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for prioritize gaps by role frequency, task centrality, transfer distance, evidence weakness, learning cost, and application timing. For Commit to the mission model · Workshop Gap Priority, keep Gap Priority connected to mechanically reviewable target-role, truthful-claim, contribution, accessibility, privacy, consent, process-state, changed-candidate, failure, repair, decision, and transfer evidence.

content.text: Three genuine evidence gaps trigger a six-month general course plan even though only one may block the candidate’s target work. The workshop variation 4 changes the candidate background, target role, jurisdiction, access need, source date, process state, and decision constraint. Competency probe: defend one bounded career decision

content.title: Commit before feedback
content.text: Choose from the stated data before opening the worked model. Record which Gap Priority condition decided your prediction.

content.label: Authority to consult after committing
content.value: NACE 2025 Career Readiness Quick Poll — Provides bounded contemporary context for skills-based graduate hiring and institutional competency integration without turning a survey into a universal employer claim.

steps.title: Defend the transferred model · Workshop Apply Learn Loop
steps.instruction:
Write the workshop evidence note for Apply Learn Loop: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — Three genuine evidence gaps trigger a six-month general course plan even though only one may block the candidate’s target work. The workshop variation 3 changes the candidate background, target role, jurisdiction, access need, source date, process state, and decision constraint. Competency probe: Interleave bounded applications, feedback signals, retrieval, projects, and plan revision without training on random rejection noise.

Evidence target: Defend the transferred model · Workshop Apply Learn Loop.
steps.why: Retrieving Apply Learn Loop through the concrete time-boxed role-readiness experiment plan case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide. For Defend the transferred model · Workshop Apply Learn Loop, keep Apply Learn Loop connected to mechanically reviewable target-role, truthful-claim, contribution, accessibility, privacy, consent, process-state, changed-candidate, failure, repair, decision, and transfer evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Interleave bounded applications, feedback signals, retrieval, projects, and plan revision without training on random rejection noise.

hints: For Defend the transferred model · Workshop Apply Learn Loop, name the learner goal, target role, jurisdiction, audience, truthful claim, contribution, source date, evidence revision, accessibility, privacy, consent, process state, decision owner, non-claim, and transfer boundary, Apply Learn Loop precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Defend the transferred model · Workshop Apply Learn Loop, trace this concrete input and predict targeting, source, evidence, contribution, access, privacy, consent, process, decision, rejection, repair, follow-up, and learner-controlled transfer evidence before reviewing it: Three genuine evidence gaps trigger a six-month general course plan even though only one may block the candidate’s target work. The workshop variation 3 changes the candidate background, target role, jurisdiction, access need, source date, process state, and decision constraint. Competency probe: Interleave bounded applications, feedback signals, retrieval, projects, and plan revision without training on random rejection noise.

For Defend the transferred model · Workshop Apply Learn Loop, challenge the documented misconception with changed-candidate, target-role, source-date, jurisdiction, audience, access, privacy, consent, process-state, interview, offer, rejection, repair, and learner-controlled transfer evidence: All application outcomes identify the exact skill gap to fix.
